elements.sent-2411-001: Several candidates for a motivic spectrum representing hermitian $K$-theory in the Morel-Voevodsky motivic stable homotopy category over schemes in which 2 is not necessarily invertible exist.
elements.sent-2411-002: This note shows that the cellular absolute motivic spectrum constructed in \cite{kumar.thesis via the geometry of orthogonal and hyperbolic Grassmannians over any scheme coincides with the motivic ring spectrum constructed in \cite{chn .}}
elements.sent-2411-003: With his creation of higher algebraic $K$-theory, Daniel Quillen constructed an interesting cohomology theory of schemes.
elements.sent-2411-004: The Morel-Voevodsky $\P^1$-stable $\A^1$-homotopy theory provides a natural home for this cohomology theory over regular schemes, similar to the classical stable homotopy category hosting complex topological $K$-theory.
elements.sent-2411-005: More precisely, higher algebraic $K$-groups of any smooth $S$-scheme $X$ are represented by a motivic ring spectrum $\KGL_S$ in the motivic stable homotopy category $\SH(S)$ for any Noetherian regular scheme $S$ \cite{voevodsky.icm , \cite{ppr , \cite{rso.strict .}}}
elements.sent-2411-006: Questions around algebraic $K$-theory, such as the existence of the motivic spectral sequence analogous to the Atiyah-Hirzebruch spectral sequence for complex topological $K$-theory, were a major driving force for motivic stable homotopy theory.
elements.sent-2411-007: The motivic spectrum $\KGL_S$ is constructed via the infinite Grassmannian, the union of the finite Grassmannians of $d$-dimensional linear subspaces of a $c+d$-dimensional affine space $\A^{c+d _S$ over $S$.}
elements.sent-2411-008: Since these are built in a suitable sense out of algebraic spheres over $S$, the motivic spectrum $\KGL_S$ is cellular \cite{dugger-isaksen.cell .}
elements.sent-2411-009: To explain the last adjective in the title, the collection $\{\KGL_S\}$ of motivic spectra is absolute in the sense that it pulls back along any morphism, that is, $f^\ast \KGL_S\simeq \KGL_R$ for any morphism $f\colon R\to S$.
elements.sent-2411-010: Hermitian $K$-theory, as conceived by Karoubi, adds the complexity of suitable bilinear forms to the vector bundles underlying algebraic $K$-theory.
elements.sent-2411-011: From the topological perspective, it is related to algebraic $K$-theory in the same way as real topological $K$-theory is related to complex topological $K$-theory.
elements.sent-2411-012: Under the additional restriction that $2$ be invertible in the regular affine Noetherian base scheme $S$, a motivic spectrum in $\SH(S)$ representing Karoubi's hermitian $K$-theory (also known as higher Grothendieck-Witt theory) was provided in \cite{hornbostel .}
elements.sent-2411-013: However, the restriction on the invertibility of $2$ prevents its absoluteness, and several authors have worked on extending the theory, in particular to the terminal scheme $\Spec(\Z)$ \cite{zbMATH07124513 , \cite{spitzweck.herm .}}
elements.sent-2411-014: The first author, in a 2020 PhD thesis \cite{kumar.thesis supervised by the second author, obtained a cellular absolute motivic spectrum $\KQ_S^\prime$ over any scheme via a geometric description.}
elements.sent-2411-015: The essential parts of the thesis have been published in \cite{kumar .}
elements.sent-2411-016: If $2$ is invertible in the Noetherian regular scheme $S$, it represents Karoubi's hermitian $K$-theory.
elements.sent-2411-017: However, a ring structure, obtained previously in \cite{lopez.thesis , as well as several structural properties, were missing, as well as an idenfication of what this motivic spectrum represents over regular Noetherian bases in which $2$ is not invertible.}
elements.sent-2411-018: This year, \cite{chn provided an absolute motivic ring spectrum $\KQ_S$ over any quasi-compact quasi-separated scheme $S$, with further desirable properties, such as absolute purity and twisted Thom isomorphisms.}
elements.sent-2411-019: Their work is based on further deep work on hermitian $K$-theory in great generality, similar to \cite{spitzweck.herm , which is partly in progress.}
elements.sent-2411-020: A geometric description, and in particular cellularity, is missing from this impressive list of properties.
elements.sent-2411-021: Theorem~\ref{thm:chn-kumar below provides that the two motivic spectra are equivalent over any quasi-compact quasi-separated scheme.}
elements.sent-2411-022: As one consequence, work by the second author with Kolderup and {\O stv{\ae r extends slice and coefficient computations to hermitian $K$-theory over Dedekind domains, including $\Z$ \cite{kro.very .}}}
elements.sent-2411-023: To prove the equivalence we use two main results.
elements.sent-2411-024: As a first result, the collaboration of Panin and Walter \cite{panin-walter.quat-borel, panin-walter.msp and work of Ananyevskiy \cite{ananyevskiy.sl provides a description of $\KQ^{\ast+(\star) (\HGr_\Z )$.}}}
elements.sent-2411-025: Using this description allows us to construct a map \begin{equation}\phi\colon \Z\times\HGr_\Z\to \Omega^{\infty+(\infty)}\Sigma^{2+(2)}\KQ_{\Z}\end{equation} of motivic spaces.
elements.sent-2411-026: Its source features $\HGr_\Z$, the quaternionic Grassmannian defined over $\Z$.
elements.sent-2411-027: The target of $\phi$ is the infinite $\P^1$-loop space of the suitably shifted hermitian $K$-theory spectrum over $\Z$.
elements.sent-2411-028: We then show this is compatible with the structure maps of $\KQ^\prime_\Z$ and $\KQ_\Z$.
elements.sent-2411-029: The resulting map $\phi\colon \KQ^\prime_\Z\to \KQ_\Z$ of motivic spectra is then an equivalence.
elements.sent-2411-030: To prove this we use as a second main result, the Grothendieck-Witt groups defined in \cite{9.III agree with the classical hermitian K-groups of Karoubi in positive degrees for fields.}
elements.sent-2411-031: Let $S$ be a qcqs (quasi-compact and quasi-separated) base scheme.
elements.sent-2411-032: We denote by $S^{t,w =S^{t-w}\wedge \mathbb{G _{m ^{w = S^{t-w+(w)}$ the motivic sphere of topological degree $t$ and weight $w$.}}}}
elements.sent-2411-033: The corresponding suspension functor on the stable motivic homotopy category $\SH(S)$ is denoted by $\Sigma^{t,w =\Sigma^{t-w+(w)}$.}
elements.sent-2411-034: For any pointed motivic space $X$ with associated motivic suspension spectrum $\Sigma^{\infty+(\infty) X$ and any motivic spectrum $\E\in \SH(S)$, set \begin{equation}\E^{t+(w)}(X)= [\Sigma^{\infty+(\infty)} X, \Sigma^{t+(w)}\E]_{\SH(S)}\end{equation} as the corresponding group of homomorphisms in $\SH(S)$.}
elements.sent-2411-035: The notation ``$\Sigma^{\infty+(\infty)}$'' may be left out for simplicity.
elements.sent-2411-036: In the particular case of motivic spheres, one associates to any motivic spectrum $\E\in\SH(S)$ the bigraded motivic homotopy group \begin{equation}\pi_{t,w}\E= \pi_{t-w+(w)}\E= [S^{t,w},\E]_{\SH(S)}\end{equation} for $t,w\in \Z$.
elements.sent-2411-037: For example, the hermitian $K$-groups of $S$ can be defined as $\KQ_{t,w (S)=\pi_{t,w}\KQ_S$.}
elements.sent-2411-038: In the literature, the notation $\GW^w_t$ is also used to denote hermitian $K$-groups; these are related to $\KQ_{t,w}$ via the indexing conventions visible in the identifications \begin{equation}\GW^{w}_{t}(S)\cong [S^{t,0}, \Sigma^{(w)}\KQ_S]=\pi_{t-2w,-w}\KQ_S=\pi_{t-w-(w)}\KQ_S.\end{equation}.
elements.sent-2411-039: For an affine scheme $X=\Spec(R)$, we use both $\KSp(X)$ and $\KSp(R)$ to denote the K-theory spectrum of the category of non-degenerate alternating (or, in the language of \cite{9.I , genuine $(-1)$-even) forms on $X$.}
elements.sent-2411-040: As a consequence, $\KSp$ denotes the corresponding spectrum valued presheaf $X\mapsto \KSp(X)$, with $\KSp_n(X)$ and $\KSp_n(R)$ short for the homotopy groups $\pi_n \KSp(X)$.
elements.sent-2411-041: Our work was generously supported by the DFG and the RCN Project No.~312472 ``Equations in Motivic Homotopy''.
elements.sent-2411-042: Recent work achieved a satisfying status for Karoubi's hermitian $K$-theory (also known as higher Grothendieck-Witt theory), which until recently was available only over base schemes in which $2$ is invertible.
elements.sent-2411-043: There exists a motivic $E_{\infty}$ ring spectrum $\KQ\in \SH(S)$ with the following properties.
elements.sent-2411-044: There exists an element $\alpha:\Sigma^{4+(4)}\unit\to \KQ$ such that multiplication with $\alpha$ is an equivalence $\KQ \simeq \Sigma^{4+(4)}\KQ$.
elements.sent-2411-045: There exists a ring map $\Forg\colon \KQ_S\to \KGL_S$ and a $\KQ_S$-module map $\Hyper\colon \KGL_S\to \KQ_S$ such that \begin{equation}\Sigma^{(1)} \KQ \xrightarrow{\eta\smash \KQ} \KQ \xrightarrow{\Forg} \KGL \xrightarrow{\Sigma^{1+(1)}\Hyper \circ \beta} \Sigma^{1+(1)}\KQ\end{equation} is a cofiber sequence.
elements.sent-2411-046: For every morphism $f\colon R\to S$ there exists a canonical equivalence $f^\ast \KQ_S\simeq \KQ_R$.
elements.sent-2411-047: For every closed embedding $i\colon R\to S$ of regular schemes of codimension $c$ and normal bundle $Ni$, the purity transformation $i^\ast\KQ_S\to \Th(Ni)\smash i^!\KQ_S$ is an equivalence.
elements.sent-2411-048: For every vector bundle $V\to S$ of rank $r$, there exists a Thom equivalence $\KQ_S\simeq \Th(V)\smash \KQ_S(\det V[-r])$.\footnote{The shifted line bundle in parentheses denotes an appropriate twist of $\KQ_S$.}
elements.sent-2411-049: If $S$ is regular and $2$ is invertible on $S$, $\KQ_S$ represents Karoubi's hermitian $K$-groups over $S$.
elements.sent-2411-050: The first statement is \cite[Remark 8.1.2]{chn ; note that $\Forg(\alpha)=\beta^4$.}
elements.sent-2411-051: The second statement follows from \cite[Corollary 8.1.7]{chn and \cite[Remark 8.1.8]{chn .}}

elements.sent-2411-116: In the Morel-Voevodsky motivic stable homotopy category of a quasi-compact quasi-separated scheme $S$, several candidates exist for a motivic spectrum representing hermitian $K$-theory.
